tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

* [EngSys] Agentic Workflows

The focus of these changes is to implement a hand-off
between the docs workflow and triage to ensure that
triage takes place after the docs issue is opened.
Because of AW platform design, the standard event
trigger for issues does not fire to prevent recursive
runs, so triage needs to be triggered directly.

This reverts commit [REDACTED].","author":{"name":"Daniel Jurek","email":"djurek@microsoft.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:44:46Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:44:46Z"}},"author":{"login":"danieljurek","id":2158838,"profile_url":"https://github.com/danieljurek","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2158838?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[EngSys] Agentic Workflow tweaks (#57793)

* [EngSys] Agentic Workflow tweaks

The focus of these changes is to tune behavior of
the issue triage and samples/docs workflows, to
remove friction and enhance functionality.

Issue Triage:
  - If labeling was successful and a package was
    identified, validate on NuGet that the package
    is not deprecated. If it is, inform the issue
    author and resolve the issue.

Samples and Docs:
  - Revise the workflow to open an issue with a
    detailed set of instructions for a coding agent
    to execute.  Previously, the workflow would
    attempt to create a PR directly, which is blocked
    by Microsoft security policies.  Each run failed
    and produced an issue with a helpful "create PR"
    link but could not be customized and broke
    issue triage requiring frequent manual intervention.

* Address PR review feedback for agentic workflows

- Fix dangling 'Conventions below' reference in docs workflow
- Fix package ID ambiguity in deprecation check (check issue body first, directory fallback, skip if ambiguous)
- Add NuGet registration pagination awareness
- Fix 'no longer published' wording to 'no longer supported'
- Strengthen snippet instructions in Implementation Guide (full lifecycle, #if !SNIPPET, expanded validation)
- Renumber deprecation lookup steps after pagination insertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: danieljurek <2158838+danieljurek@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:22:07Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:22:07Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Fix flaky HttpClientTransportReferenceCountingTests under coverage (#57790)

* Fix flaky HttpClientTransportReferenceCountingTests under coverage (#57789)

Replace Task.Delay timing with deterministic SemaphoreSlim/ManualResetEventSlim
synchronization to ensure requests are in-flight before Update() is called.

Closes #57789

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap synchronization in try/finally to prevent test hangs on failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single CancellationTokenSource timeout for semaphore waits

Replaces per-iteration 10s timeouts with a single overall 10s deadline
so failure mode is bounded at 10s total, not N*10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use single 5s CTS deadline for all synchronization waits

Wire the CancellationToken into both the semaphore waits and the
handler's ManualResetEventSlim.Wait so everything unblocks cleanly
on timeout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use LongRunning tasks to avoid thread pool starvation in sync variant

In sync mode, each request blocks a thread pool thread. With 10
concurrent requests, the pool needs time to grow, causing timeouts
on CI agents with few cores. Using TaskCreationOptions.LongRunning
creates dedicated threads, avoiding starvation. Timeout set to 10s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"m-nash","email":"64171366+m-nash@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:10:34Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-03T23:10:34Z"}},"author":{"login":"m-nash","id":64171366,"profile_url":"https://github.com/m-nash","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Add TestDependsOnDependency for packages with cross-service dependents (#57685)

* Add TestDependsOnDependency to CI files for packages with cross-service dependents

When a package is depended on by test projects in other service directories,
changes to that package can break downstream consumers (as seen with the
Storage TypeSpec migration breaking DataFactory tests).

This change:
- Adds TestDependsOnDependency to 38 CI files covering 50 packages that have
  cross-service test dependents. When these packages change, the CI pipeline
  automatically discovers and runs all downstream dependent tests with
  UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true.
- Adds a validation step to CodeChecks.ps1 that detects when a package has
  cross-service dependents but is not covered by TestDependsOnDependency.
  In CI mode it fails with instructions; with -SkipDiffValidation it auto-fixes.
- Adds unit tests for the new validation and auto-fix functions.

Ref: #57679

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ForEach-Object return bug in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Replace ForEach-Object pipeline with foreach loop so that skipping
the current service directory uses 'continue' instead of 'return',
which would have exited the entire function early.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused pkgToSvc mapping in Get-MissingTestDependsOnDependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-platform path handling for ProjectReference on Linux

Normalize backslashes to forward slashes before calling
GetFileNameWithoutExtension, since Linux does not treat
backslash as a path separator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Join-String with -join for PS 5.1 compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use UTF-8 no-BOM encoding when writing CI YAML files

Avoids PS 5.1 defaulting to UTF-16 with Set-Content, which would
introduce encoding changes and potentially break YAML tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CODEOWNERS entry for CI pipeline config files

Require review from @Azure/azure-sdk-write-net-core for any changes
to ci*.yml files under sdk/, ensuring CI pipeline configuration
changes are reviewed by the core team.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI

Smaller batches run CodeChecks on fewer service directories per job,
reducing the risk of timeouts when many services are changed. This
creates more parallel jobs but each finishes faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5 for faster CI"

This reverts commit [REDACTED].

* Reduce PRJobBatchSize from 10 to 5

The Analyze step runs CodeChecks (codegen + snippets + API export)
sequentially for each service in a batch. Heavy services like
websites and compute take 8-15 min each, so batches of 6+ services
can exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 5 per batch keeps
each job under the timeout while parallelizing across more agents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip codegen/snippets/API export when only ci*.yml files changed

When CodeChecks runs for a service directory where only CI pipeline
config files (ci*.yml) changed, skip the expensive GenerateCode,
Update-Snippets, and Export-API steps since they cannot be affected
by CI config changes. This significantly reduces Analyze job time
for PRs that modify many ci.yml files across service directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add try/catch fallback and error handling tests for Test-OnlyCiConfigChanged

Wraps the function in try/catch so any unexpected error (corrupt repo,
permissions, etc.) falls back to returning false (run full codegen).
Adds tests for non-git directory, nonexistent path, and empty input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Only skip codegen for ci*.yml-only changes in PR builds

Release and CI push pipelines should always run the full codegen,
snippets, and API export checks. Guard the optimization behind
BUILD_REASON == PullRequest so it only applies to PR validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce NumOfTestProjectsPerJob from 20 to 10

The dependency test batches run test projects sequentially. Heavy
test suites like Storage, KeyVault, and Search can cause batches
of 20 projects to exceed the 60-min job timeout. Reducing to 10
creates more parallel jobs that each finish faster.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce test PRJobBatchSize from 20 to 10

The test matrix batches packages alphabetically with no weighting.
With 20 per batch, some batches get heavy test suites that exceed
the 60-min timeout on macOS agents. Reducing to 10 creates more
parallel test jobs with fewer packages each.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ignore ContainerApps Job tests and revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob

Ignore ContainerAppJobTests.CreateOrUpdate and
ContainerAppJobApiTests.CreateOrUpdate — these fail with
TestRecordingMismatchException due to api-version mismatch
(2023-05-01 recorded vs 2025-07-01 source-built) when running
with UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true. Tracked in #57787.

Revert NumOfTestProjectsPerJob back to 20 as the change had no
effect — this parameter controls the TestDependsOnDependency
split, not the main PR test batching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"m-nash","email":"64171366+m-nash@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-03T21:38:43Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-03T21:38:43Z"}},"author":{"login":"m-nash","id":64171366,"profile_url":"https://github.com/m-nash","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}]

{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Increment package version after release of Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright (#57806)","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-06T15:39:45Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-06T15:39:45Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"},"stats":{"additions":11,"deletions":1,"total":12},"files":[{"filename":"sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/CHANGELOG.md","status":"modified","additions":10,"changes":10},{"filename":"sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/src/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright.csproj","status":"modified","additions":1,"deletions":1,"changes":2}]}

{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[EngSys] Agentic Workflows (#57804)

* [EngSys] Agentic Workflows

The focus of these changes is to implement a hand-off
between the docs workflow and triage to ensure that
triage takes place after the docs issue is opened.
Because of AW platform design, the standard event
trigger for issues does not fire to prevent recursive
runs, so triage needs to be triggered directly.

* Applying PR feedback","author":{"name":"Jesse Squire","email":"jesse.squire@gmail.com","date":"2026-04-04T19:49:07Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-04T19:49:07Z"}},"author":{"login":"jsquire","id":913445,"profile_url":"https://github.com/jsquire","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}

resource.text:
# Release History

## 1.1.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)

### Features Added

### Breaking Changes

### Bugs Fixed

### Other Changes

## 1.1.0-beta.1 (2026-04-06)

### Features Added

- Upgraded API version to `2026-02-01-preview`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspaceData` added property `Identity` of type `ManagedServiceIdentity`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspacePatch` added property `Identity` of type `ManagedServiceIdentity`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties` added property `Reporting` of type `PlaywrightEnablementStatus?`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties` added property `StorageUri` of type `Uri`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties` added property `Reporting` of type `PlaywrightEnablementStatus?`.
  - `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties` added property `StorageUri` of type `Uri`.

## 1.0.0 (2025-09-05)

### Features Added

First stable release for api-version 2025-09-01.

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2025-07-30)

### Features Added

This package follows the [new Azure SDK guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/general_introduction.html), and provides many core capabilities:

    - Support MSAL.NET, Azure.Identity is out of box for supporting MSAL.NET.
    - Support [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) for distributed tracing.
    - HTTP pipeline with custom policies.
    - Better error-handling.
    - Support uniform telemetry across all languages.

This package is a Public Preview version, so expect incompatible changes in subsequent releases as we improve the product. To provide feedback, submit an issue in our [Azure SDK for .NET GitHub repo](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).

> NOTE: For more information about unified authentication, please refer to [Microsoft Azure Identity documentation for .NET](https://docs.microsoft.com//dotnet/api/overview/azure/identity-readme?view=azure-dotnet).

# Microsoft Azure Playwright management client library for .NET

Microsoft Playwright is a fully managed service for end-to-end testing built on top of Playwright. With Playwright, you can automate end-to-end tests to ensure your web applications work the way you expect it to, across different web browsers and operating systems. The service abstracts the complexity and infrastructure for running Playwright tests with high parallelization.

This library follows the [new Azure SDK guidelines](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/general_introduction.html), and provides many core capabilities:

    - Support MSAL.NET, Azure.Identity is out of box for supporting MSAL.NET.
    - Support [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) for distributed tracing.
    - HTTP pipeline with custom policies.
    - Better error-handling.
    - Support uniform telemetry across all languages.

## Getting started 

### Install the package

Install the Microsoft Azure Playwright management library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):

```dotnetcli
dotnet add package Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright
```

### Prerequisites

* You must have an [Microsoft Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/).

### Authenticate the Client

To create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, see the [quickstart guide here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/mgmt_quickstart.md).

## Key concepts

Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html)

## Documentation

Documentation is available to help you learn how to use this package:

- [Quickstart](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/mgmt_quickstart.md).
- [API References](https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/?view=azure-dotnet).
- [Authentication](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md).

## Examples

Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations
- [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples)

## Troubleshooting

-   File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).
-   Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.

## Next steps

For more information about Microsoft Azure SDK, see [this website](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/).

## Contributing

For details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing
guide][cg].

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions
require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring
that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use
your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine
whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately
(for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the
bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories
using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For
more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact
<opencode@microsoft.com> with any other questions or comments.

<!-- LINKS -->
[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md
[coc]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/
[coc_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/
